source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Worker injury from uncontrolled sandblasting nozzle
actions_taken: Worker evacuated to Terrebonne General Medical Center
BSEE Lafayette District notified at 1811 hours on February 2, 2020
Violation citation INC G-110 issued to Shell Offshore Inc.
event.summary: On February 2, 2020, at approximately 1600 hours, a sandblaster at Garden Banks 128-A sustained a laceration to the right bicep when he lost control of a sandblasting nozzle pressurized at 85 lbs while standing on an improperly secured scaffold board placed across two vessels. The board shifted during repositioning, causing the worker to lose his grip on the nozzle. The nozzle whipped and became lodged under the worker's right arm pit, blasting through his clothing before the dead-man switch could shut down the system.
recommendations: Incorporate do's and don'ts into Cable Rigging training course to prohibit laying boards from vessel to vessel
Incorporate depressurization of hoses prior to moving or repositioning as best practice in Abrasive Blasting Procedure
Document hose depressurization requirement on Job Safety Analysis
Designate a specific employee to maintain constant visual contact with workers at heights
Construct proper scaffolds between vessels instead of using unsecured boards

hazards.name: Working at elevation on unstable platform
Uncontrolled nozzle whip from pressurized hose

threats.name: Loss of grip on blasting nozzle due to platform shift
Improper scaffold board securing and placement
Dead-man switch failure to immediately shut down system

consequences.name: Worker laceration and soft tissue injury
Lost work time and regulatory violation

controls.name: Proper scaffold construction between vessels
controls.barrier_role: Prevent platform instability and worker loss of balance
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to eliminate hazard of unstable working platform

supporting_text: A scaffold could have been constructed between the vessels to conduct blasting operations in a safe and workmanlike manner
The lessee did not follow the Danos Abrasive Blasting Operations – 6.2.2.8 Working at Elevation Policy which states, working from scaffolds, not ladders or other antiquated elevating items

controls.name: Proper securing of scaffold board with rope and tie-downs
controls.barrier_role: Prevent board shifting and worker loss of balance
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent platform movement during work

supporting_text: During the BSEE onsite investigation, an inspector was able to easily move the board due to the board not being secured properly
The board was tied with rope from the ends of the board to outlets located at the bottom of the vessels
As the sandblaster attempted to reposition himself, the board shifted causing him to lose his grip on the blasting nozzle

controls.name: Dead-man switch on blasting nozzle
controls.barrier_role: Shut down pressurized system when nozzle is released
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to limit duration of uncontrolled nozzle discharge

supporting_text: The sandblaster stated once he lost his grip on the blasting nozzle, the dead-man switch delayed prior to shutting down
Equipment Failure – inoperable equipment safety device: the dead-man switch delayed prior to shutting down

controls.name: Designated visual contact person during working at heights
controls.barrier_role: Monitor worker safety and detect hazards during elevated work
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to provide continuous hazard monitoring

supporting_text: According to the Job Safety Analysis and the Working at Heights Fall Rescue Plan, constant visual contact would be maintained on the workers. There were no names listed on the Job Safety Analysis or any other documents to suggest an employee was designated to maintain a constant visual
The hazards of failing to secure the boards and placing the boards across two vessels was not detected

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) hazard identification
controls.barrier_role: Identify and document work hazards prior to task execution
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative control to prevent hazards through planning

supporting_text: The hazards of failing to secure the boards and placing the boards across two vessels was not detected

controls.name: Hose depressurization procedure before repositioning
controls.barrier_role: Eliminate pressure in hose before worker movement to prevent uncontrolled discharge
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to reduce consequence severity

supporting_text: incorporating depressurization of hoses prior to moving or repositioning yourself as best practice in the Abrasive Blasting Procedure and documented on the Job Safety Analysis

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
